output.summary: Add a presetFilters attribute (object mapping taxonomy slugs to arrays of term slugs) so editors can pre-select filters when placing the block. On page load, if no URL filter params exist, the preset values are used as defaults. When displayFilters is false, the active filter pill bar is also hidden since users cannot interactively remove filters.

fileChanges.details: Add new attribute: "presetFilters": { "type": "object", "default": {} }. The object maps taxonomy query-var slugs (e.g. "topic", "audience", "resource-type") to arrays of term slugs (e.g. { "topic": ["finance", "health"] }).

fileChanges.description: Add editor UI for preset filters and update type definition
fileChanges.details: 1) Add presetFilters to ArchiveAttributes type as Record<string, string[]>. 2) Add a new PanelBody 'Preset Filters' in InspectorControls. Use a REST API call (useSelect or apiFetch) to load taxonomy terms for the current blockVariation's taxonomies. Render a multi-select or checkbox list per taxonomy so editors can pick which terms are pre-selected. 3) Pass presetFilters as a data attribute on the section element so the FilteredServerSideRender preview reflects them. 4) When blockVariation changes, clear presetFilters that reference taxonomies not in the new variation.

fileChanges.description: Apply preset filters to WP_Query and conditionally hide active filter bar
fileChanges.details: 1) Add @var object $presetFilters docblock. 2) After collecting $selected_options from URL query vars, if a taxonomy has NO URL selection but has a preset value in $presetFilters, merge the preset values in as defaults. This means URL params always override presets. 3) Set $has_selected_option = true if any preset filters are active (so the query applies them). 4) When rendering the active filter pills section, add a condition: only show the .archive-active-filters bar if $displayFilters is true (currently it only checks $show_filters which already factors in displayFilters, but we must also suppress the bar when displayFilters is false even if presetFilters cause $has_selected_option to be true). 5) Output presetFilters as a data attribute (JSON-encoded) on the section element so the JS can distinguish preset vs user-selected filters. 6) Also pre-populate the hidden input values for each taxonomy filter select so the form state matches the preset.

fileChanges.description: Initialize filter state from preset filters on page load
fileChanges.details: 1) On init, read data-preset-filters from the section element (JSON). 2) In the loadFiltersFromUrl function, when no URL params exist for a taxonomy, fall back to preset values from the data attribute. 3) The buildButtons function already reads from hidden inputs, so if PHP pre-populates them correctly, the active pills will render on initial load. 4) When 'Clear All' is clicked, clear to empty (not back to presets) — clearing should truly clear. This matches expected UX: preset filters set initial state but users can remove them.

attributeChanges.name: presetFilters
attributeChanges.description: Pre-selected taxonomy filters applied on initial page load. Maps taxonomy query-var slugs to arrays of term slugs, e.g. { "topic": ["finance", "health"], "audience": ["students"] }. URL parameters override presets. Only taxonomies relevant to the current blockVariation are used.

designDecisions.question: Should 'Clear All' reset to preset filters or clear everything?
designDecisions.recommendation: Clear everything (empty state). Presets define the initial load state, but once the user interacts, they should have full control. This is the standard UX pattern for preset/default filters.

designDecisions.question: Should the active filter pills bar be visible when displayFilters is false but presetFilters are active?
designDecisions.recommendation: No. Per the user's request: when displayFilters is false, hide the active filter bar entirely. The pills are not interactive if filters are hidden, so showing them serves no purpose.

designDecisions.question: Should URL parameters override preset filters or merge with them?
designDecisions.recommendation: Override per taxonomy. If the URL has ?topic=finance, use only that value for the topic taxonomy, ignoring any presetFilters.topic values. This gives users clean control via URL sharing and avoids confusing merged states.

designDecisions.question: What editor UI pattern should be used for selecting preset filter terms?
designDecisions.recommendation: Use FormTokenField (tag-style input) per taxonomy in a dedicated 'Preset Filters' PanelBody. FormTokenField supports typeahead from loaded terms and is the standard WordPress pattern for multi-select taxonomy inputs. Show this panel only when displayFilters is true or always (since presets affect the query regardless of whether filter UI is shown). Recommend: always show it, since presets can narrow results even without visible filters.

designDecisions.question: Should preset filters be variation-aware (reset when blockVariation changes)?
designDecisions.recommendation: Yes. When blockVariation changes, clear presetFilters entirely since taxonomy slugs differ between variations (e.g. 'topic' for posts vs 'audience' for resources). Stale keys would be harmless (ignored) but clearing avoids editor confusion.
